name: Dummy Deck
description: Sandbox for testing new technical phrases before migration.

cards.sideA: Yeah, eso es seguro.

sideB.text: Yeah, that's for sure.
Yeah, that's certain.
Yeah, that's a sure thing.

cards.sideA: Ok, las imágenes no se están escalando correctamente, tenemos que ajustarlas mejor.

sideB.text: Okay, the images aren't scaling correctly; we need to adjust them better.
Ok, the images are not scaling properly, we have to adjust them better.
Alright, the images aren't scaling right, we need to fine-tune them.

cards.sideA: No estoy seguro que eso vaya a funcionar a largo plazo.

sideB.text: I'm not sure that's going to work in the long run.
I'm not sure that will work long-term.
I don't think that'll work out in the long term.

cards.sideA: No esperaba eso.

sideB.text: I didn't expect that.
I wasn't expecting that.
That was unexpected.

cards.sideA: Depende en lo que pase después.

sideB.text: Depends on what's next.
It depends on what happens next.
That depends on what happens afterwards.

cards.sideA: OK volvamos al punto principal.

sideB.text: Let's get back to the main point.
Okay, let's return to the main point.
Alright, let's go back to the main topic.

cards.sideA: Las cosas no fueron como planeamos.

sideB.text: Things didn't go as planned.
Things didn't go according to plan.
Things didn't turn out the way we planned.

cards.sideA: Es ok por ahora.

sideB.text: It's good enough for now.
It's fine for the time being.
It's okay for now.

cards.sideA: Gente del área técnica, negocios, testers, etc.

sideB.text: People from the technical side, business, testers, etc.
Technical people, business folks, testers, and so on.
Tech guys, business people, QA testers, etc.

cards.sideA: Y tuve una gran mejora en mi comunicación.

sideB.text: And I had a huge improvement in my communication.
And I improved my communication skills a lot.
And I made a big improvement in my communication.

cards.sideA: La habidad de focalizar en lo que importa.

sideB.text: The ability to focus on what matters.
The skill to focus on what's important.
Being able to focus on the important stuff.

cards.sideA: Necesito focalizarme solo en lo que importa.

sideB.text: I need to focus only on what matters.
I just need to focus on what's important.
I have to focus exclusively on what really matters.

cards.sideA: Así tenemos la info en la app, tanto de las líneas como de los headers.

sideB.text: This way we have the info in the app, from both the lines and the headers.
That way we get the data in the app, both from the lines and headers.
So we have the info in the app, for both the lines and the headers.

cards.sideA: Así enviamos la data, tanto de los headers como de las lineas.

sideB.text: This way we send the data, for both the headers and the lines.
That way we send the data, from the headers as well as the lines.
So we send the data, both from the headers and the lines.

cards.sideA: Si tenemos el valor de A, entonces multiplicamos con el valor de B.

sideB.text: If we have the value of A, then we multiply it by the value of B.
If we get A's value, we then multiply it by B's value.
If we have A, then we multiply by B.

cards.sideA: Si no tiene estatus de retorno, entonces multiplicamos con el valor de C.

sideB.text: If it doesn't have a return status, then we multiply it by the value of C.
If there is no return status, we multiply by the value of C.
If it lacks a return status, then we multiply by C's value.

cards.sideA: Cuando la linea es de retorno multiplicamos por la cantidad en negativo.

sideB.text: When it's a return line, we multiply by the negative amount.
When the line is a return, we multiply by the negative quantity.
If it's a return line, we multiply by the amount in negative.

cards.sideA: Y si la linea no es de retorno, multiplicamos por la cantidad en positivo.

sideB.text: And if the line isn't a return, we multiply by the positive amount.
And if it's not a return line, we multiply by the positive quantity.
And if it is not a return line, we multiply by the amount in positive.

cards.sideA: Solo estaba estandarizando el código.

sideB.text: I was just standardizing the code.
I was only standardizing the code.
I was simply making the code standard.

cards.sideA: Ese cambio es solo para estandarizar el código.

sideB.text: That change is just to standardize the code.
That change is only for standardizing the code.
This change is just to make the code standard.

cards.sideA: Estamos multiplicado el valor por 1 negativo cuando la linea es de retorno.

sideB.text: We are multiplying the value by negative one when the line is a return.
We multiply the value by minus one when it's a return line.
We're multiplying the amount by negative one if it's a return line.

cards.sideA: Y si no es de retorno, multiplicamos por 1 positivo, para mantener el valor original.

sideB.text: And if it's not a return, we multiply by positive one to keep the original value.
And if it isn't a return, we multiply by plus one to maintain the original value.
And if it's not a return line, we multiply by a positive one to keep the original amount.

cards.sideA: Sí, estamos invirtiendo el signo a negativo o positivo.

sideB.text: Yes, we are flipping the sign to negative or positive.
Yes, we're inverting the sign to negative or positive.
Yeah, we are reversing the sign to negative or positive.

cards.sideA: Sí, estamos invirtiendo el signo a positivo o negativo segun el caso.

sideB.text: Yes, we are flipping the sign to positive or negative depending on the case.
Yes, we're inverting the sign to positive or negative as the case may be.
Yeah, we change the sign to positive or negative depending on the situation.

cards.sideA: Sí, cuando agregamos el signo negativo, el valor total cambia a negativo también.

sideB.text: Yes, when we add the negative sign, the total value changes to negative too.
Yes, when we append the negative sign, the total amount also becomes negative.
Yeah, by adding the minus sign, the total value turns negative as well.

cards.sideA: Puedes usar estas, pero que no sean esas.

sideB.text: You can use these, but not those.
You can use these ones, just not those ones.
You may use these, as long as they aren't those.

cards.sideA: Sí, pero que no sean esas.

sideB.text: Yes, but not those.
Yeah, just not those ones.
Yes, as long as it's not those.

cards.sideA: ¿Que hay afuera de la simulación?

sideB.text: What's outside the simulation?
What is outside of the simulation?
What lies beyond the simulation?

cards.sideA: Tiene lógica que podría ser util.

sideB.text: It has logic that could be useful.
It contains logic that might be handy.
It has some logic that could come in handy.

cards.sideA: En la practica es usado para evaluar la existencia de algo.

sideB.text: In practice, it is used to evaluate the existence of something.
In practice, it's used to check if something exists.
Practically speaking, it's used to assess the existence of something.

cards.sideA: My listening skill... no era tan buena.

sideB.text: My listening skill wasn't that good.
My listening skills weren't that great.
My listening wasn't very good.

cards.sideA: Tenía las bases, pero tuve que mejorar un monton.

sideB.text: I had the basics, but I had to improve a lot.
I had the foundation, but I needed to improve a ton.
I knew the basics, but I had to get a lot better.

cards.sideA: Tuve que aprender a articular mis oraciones correctamente.

sideB.text: I had to learn to articulate my sentences correctly.
I had to learn how to articulate my sentences properly.
I needed to learn to structure my sentences correctly.

cards.sideA: Tuve que aprender a hacerme entender con diferente tipo de gente.

sideB.text: I had to learn to make myself understood with different types of people.
I had to learn how to get my point across to different kinds of people.
I needed to learn to make myself clear to different types of people.

cards.sideA: El comando varia un poco dependiendo del sistema operativo usado.

sideB.text: The command varies a bit depending on the operating system used.
The command changes slightly depending on the OS used.
The command is a little different depending on the operating system you use.

cards.sideA: El comando varia dependiendo de la consola de comando usada.

sideB.text: The command varies depending on the command console used.
The command differs depending on the terminal used.
The command changes based on the command prompt used.

cards.sideA: El comando depende de la consola y sistema operativo.

sideB.text: The command depends on the console and operating system.
The command relies on the terminal and OS.
The command is determined by the console and operating system.

cards.sideA: A causa de los espacios algunos caracteres invisibles son agregados.

sideB.text: Because of the spaces, some invisible characters are added.
Due to the spaces, some invisible characters get added.
Some invisible characters are added because of the spaces.

cards.sideA: Caracteres invisibles son agregados y causan problemas.

sideB.text: Invisible characters are added and cause problems.
Invisible characters get added and they cause issues.
Invisible characters are inserted, causing problems.

cards.sideA: Los espacios causan problemas por caracteres invisibles.

sideB.text: Spaces cause problems because of invisible characters.
The spaces cause issues due to invisible characters.
Spaces create problems because of some invisible characters.

cards.sideA: Son diferentes tipos de operadores de Null.

sideB.text: They are different types of null operators.
Those are different kinds of null operators.
They're various types of null operators.

cards.sideA: Ya que en los requerimientos no estaba eso, no lo agregue.

sideB.text: Since that wasn't in the requirements, I didn't add it.
Because it wasn't in the requirements, I didn't include it.
As it was not in the requirements, I didn't add it.

cards.sideA: Ya que en el spreadsheet no estaba, no lo considere en este release.

sideB.text: Since it wasn't in the spreadsheet, I didn't consider it for this release.
Because it wasn't on the spreadsheet, I didn't include it in this release.
As it was missing from the spreadsheet, I didn't consider it for this release.

cards.sideA: El sistema procesa la información rápidamente ahora.

sideB.text: The system processes the information quickly now.
The system handles the data rapidly now.
The system processes the info swiftly now.

cards.sideA: Nosotros casi nunca usamos esa librería antigua.

sideB.text: We rarely use that old library.
We almost never use that legacy library.
We seldom use that outdated library.

cards.sideA: La base de datos está casi llena en este momento.

sideB.text: The database is almost full right now.
The database is nearly full at the moment.
The DB is almost completely full right now.

cards.sideA: El cliente devolvió el producto defectuoso a la tienda.

sideB.text: The customer returned the defective product to the store.
The customer brought the faulty product back to the store.
The customer took the broken item back to the shop.

cards.note:
**[210]**

Use [customer] when referring to someone buying a physical product in a retail transaction. Do not use ~client~ here.

cards.sideA: Tenemos una reunión con nuestro cliente más importante mañana.

sideB.text: We have a meeting with our most important client tomorrow.
We are meeting with our top client tomorrow.
We have a session with our biggest client tomorrow.

cards.note:
**[210]**

Use [client] for professional services (software dev, consulting, law) where there is an ongoing relationship, not ~customer~.

cards.sideA: El fondo de la aplicación debería ser blanco puro.

sideB.text: The background of the application should be pure white.
The app's background needs to be solid white.
The background for the app should be entirely white.

cards.note:
**[210]**

[White] refers strictly to the color itself.

cards.sideA: Si dejas el campo en blanco, la validación fallará.

sideB.text: If you leave the field blank, the validation will fail.
The validation will fail if you leave the input blank.
If that field is left blank, it will fail the validation.

cards.note:
**[210]**

When a field or document is empty, we use [blank], never the literal translation ~white~.

cards.sideA: ¿En qué dirección debo mover este componente en la pantalla?

sideB.text: In which direction should I move this component on the screen?
What direction should I drag this component on the screen?
Which direction do I need to move this UI component?

cards.note:
**[210]**

[Direction] describes a path of movement or orientation.

cards.sideA: Necesito actualizar la dirección de envío del usuario en la base de datos.

sideB.text: I need to update the user's shipping address in the database.
I have to update the shipping address for the user in the DB.
I need to change the user's delivery address in the database.

cards.note:
**[210]**

When referring to a physical location data (street, city) or digital endpoint (IP, email), use [address], never ~direction~.
